title: chenopodium atriplicis
intro: Chenopodium atriplicis, also known as common lamb's quarters, is a type of annual herbaceous plant belonging to the Amaranthaceae family. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Chenopodium atriplicis include the relief of symptoms associated with arthritis, colds, and fever, as well as its potential to lower blood sugar levels. Chenopodium atriplicis can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, and its leaves can also be used in salads or as a cooking green.
